Transpile the provided C++ implementation into a functionally equivalent implementation in Mojo.

```cpp
int levenshtein(std::string a, std::string b){ int n=(int)a.size(); int m=(int)b.size(); std::vector<int> prev(m+1), cur(m+1); for(int j=0;j<=m;j++) prev[j]=j; for(int i=1;i<=n;i++){ cur[0]=i; for(int j=1;j<=m;j++){ int cost=(a[i-1]==b[j-1])?0:1; int d=prev[j]+1; int ins=cur[j-1]+1; int sub=prev[j-1]+cost; int mn=d; if(ins<mn) mn=ins; if(sub<mn) mn=sub; cur[j]=mn; } for(int j=0;j<=m;j++) prev[j]=cur[j]; } return prev[m]; }
```

mojo_driver:
def main():
    print(levenshtein("kitten", "sitting"))
    print(levenshtein("flaw", "lawn"))
    print(levenshtein("abc", "abc"))

```cpp
std::string run_length_encode(std::string s){ int n=(int)s.size(); std::string result=""; int i=0; while(i<n){ char c=s[i]; int run=1; while(i+run<n && s[i+run]==c) run++; result+=c; result+=std::to_string(run); i+=run; } return result; }
```

    print(run_length_encode("aaabbc"))
    print(run_length_encode("abc"))
    print(run_length_encode("wwwww"))

```cpp
uint64_t swap_nibbles(uint64_t b){ uint64_t lo=b&0x0FULL; uint64_t hi=(b>>4)&0x0FULL; return (lo<<4)|hi; }
```
